action.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: penguin, blueberry, milkshake, hummingbird, mask, eyelash, pastry, hubcap, cranberry.
Your opponent's team words are: fishhooks, crate, cello, lollipop, underwear, anaconda, vodka, jar.
Distractor words are: fern, bug, undershirt, egg, motorboat, doughnut, pillow.
The kill words are: octopus.
action.label: context

action.content:
<reasoning>
Let's tackle this clue-giving problem. The user's team has words like penguin, blueberry, milkshake, hummingbird, mask, eyelash, pastry, hubcap, cranberry. I need to find a single-word clue that relates to as many of these as possible without being one of the words themselves or containing parts of them.

First, let's look for common themes. Penguins are birds, blueberries and cranberries are fruits, milkshake is a drink, hummingbird is another bird, mask could be protective or theatrical, eyelash is part of the eye, pastry is food, hubcap is part of a car. Hmm, not obvious. Maybe think of categories. Fruits: blueberry, cranberry. Birds: penguin, hummingbird. Food/drinks: milkshake, pastry. Other: mask, eyelash, hubcap. Maybe there's a connection between some of these.

Wait, penguin and hummingbird are both birds. Blueberry and cranberry are berries. Milkshake is a drink, maybe related to berries? Not sure. Mask and eyelash are both facial features. Pastry is food, hubcap is a car part. Maybe not. Alternatively, think of parts of things. Hubcap is part of a car, eyelash is part of the eye. Mask could be part of a costume. Maybe "part" is a clue? But the clue can't be part of the words. Wait, the clue has to be semantically related but not contain parts. So maybe "part" is too vague.

Alternatively, think of colors. Blueberry is blue, penguin is often associated with blue/white. But mask could be any color. Not sure. Maybe "blue" as a clue? But blueberry is already blue. Maybe "bird" for penguin and hummingbird. But that only covers two. "Food" for blueberry, cranberry, milkshake, pastry. That's four. Let's check: blueberry (fruit), cranberry (fruit), milkshake (beverage), pastry (food). That's four. Maybe "food" as the clue? But the clue can't be "food" if any of the target words are food? Wait, the clue just can't be one of the words in the lists. "Food" isn't in the lists, so that's okay. Let's see: TARGETS would be blueberry,
action.label: response

content.message: Your answer contained more than two lines, please only give one clue and your targets on two separate lines.
